name: From Mengabril to the Condado de Medellín — circular
name_original: De Mengabril al Condado de Medellín — circular
region_intro: Jogging routes around Mengabril traverse a landscape characterized by the plains of the Guadiana River and the historic area surrounding Medellín. The region features gentle river valleys and some rolling hills, particularly around areas like Alto de la Ortiga. These physical features provide varied terrain suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Mengabril The most popular jogging route is Views of the Castle by the Guadiana River — circular from Medellín, an 8.2 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This circular route offers views of the historic castle and the Guadiana River. Another top favourite among local runners is Alto de la Ortiga Loop Trail, a difficult 12.4 miles (19.9 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain, traversing varied terrain. Local runners also love the Castillo de Medellín — circular por las Vegas Altas del Guadiana, a 2.0 miles (3.2 km) trail leading through the plains of the Vegas Altas del Guadiana, often completed in about 22 minutes. Jogging around Mengabril is defined by river plains, historic castles, and rolling hills. The network offers options for easy runs as well as more challenging routes with elevation. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 260 runners have used komoot to explore Mengabril's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Mengabril?
A: There are over 45 running routes available in the Mengabril area, offering a variety of distances and difficulties for all types of runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Mengabril?
A: The jogging routes around Mengabril traverse a landscape characterized by the plains of the Guadiana River and the historic area surrounding Medellín. You'll find gentle river valleys and some rolling hills, particularly around areas like Alto de la Ortiga, providing varied terrain.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Mengabril?
A: Yes, Mengabril offers several easy running options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280334767/castillo-de-medellin-circular-por-las-vegas-altas-del-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Medellín — circular por las Vegas Altas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; is a 2.0-mile (3.2 km) trail that's great for an easy run.

Q: Where can I find challenging running trails near Mengabril?
A: For a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284016642/alto-de-la-ortiga-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Ortiga Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 12.4-mile (19.9 km) path with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284550272/from-mengabril-to-the-condado-de-medellin-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Mengabril to the Condado de Medellín — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.3 miles (15.1 km) long.

Q: Are there circular running routes in the Mengabril area?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282803562/views-of-the-castle-by-the-guadiana-river-circular-from-medellin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Castle by the Guadiana River — circular from Medellín&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.2-mile (13.3 km) loop offering scenic views.

Q: Are the jogging routes around Mengabril dog-friendly?
A: Many outdoor trails in the Mengabril region are suitable for running with dogs, especially those away from urban centers. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of local regulations and wildlife.

Q: Are there family-friendly running options in Mengabril?
A: Absolutely. The easier, flatter routes, particularly those along the river plains, are ideal for families. These trails often have less elevation and are shorter, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks can I see while jogging in Mengabril?
A: While running, you can encounter several historical and scenic points of interest. Look out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5725452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Theater and Castle of Medellín&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medellín Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and various viewpoints offering &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of Medellín Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available near the running trails in Mengabril?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the towns and villages that serve as starting points for many routes, such as Medellín. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route pages on komoot.

Q: How can I access running routes in Mengabril using public transport?
A: While public transport options might be limited directly to trailheads, major towns like Medellín, which is close to Mengabril, are typically accessible by regional bus services. From these towns, you can often reach the start of many running routes on foot.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Mengabril?
A: Over 260 runners have used komoot to explore Mengabril's varied terrain. They often praise the region for its mix of river plains, historic castles, and rolling hills, providing diverse running experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Mengabril?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging in Mengabril, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable, while winters are typically mild but can be damp.
tips: We are at the foot of the Castillo del Condado de Medellín, rolling along a wide dirt road near the beautiful 17th century bridge in Medellín. At the foot of the castle is the Roman theater and the church of Santiago Apóstol. The visit is highly recommended (€2 adults)
From this point there are incredible views of the Castle of Medellín which is located at the top of the hill where it is located in the same town.
